name: App Center (Microsoft)
what_it_does: Microsoft's mobile DevOps platform with REST API for automating builds, distributing apps to testers, collecting crash reports, and sending push notifications — supporting iOS, Android, React Native, and Xamarin.
use_cases: Automated CI/CD builds triggered via API for iOS and Android apps
Beta distribution — releasing test builds to tester groups via API-triggered distribution
Crash reporting analytics — querying crash groups and error details for incident triage agents
Push notification campaigns from agents to segmented user groups
App version management and tracking across multiple platforms
not_for: Full backend-as-a-service (no database, auth, or storage — App Center is DevOps/distribution only)
Expo/React Native projects already using EAS (EAS is purpose-built for Expo ecosystem)
Teams requiring deep analytics beyond crashes and distributions
Long-term planning — App Center has been in maintenance mode with uncertain future
best_when: You're developing iOS/Android apps cross-platform (including Xamarin/.NET) and need integrated CI/CD, crash reporting, and beta distribution in one Microsoft-hosted service.
avoid_when: You're exclusively using Expo/React Native (use EAS instead), or you need a platform with an active development roadmap — App Center's future is uncertain as Microsoft transitions to Azure DevOps.

auth.notes: API token in X-API-Token header. Tokens are user-scoped or app-scoped. No OAuth for third-party integrations — each user generates their own token.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: unlimited builds, 240 minutes/month build time, 500 push notifications/month
pricing.paid_tiers: Basic $49/user/month for unlimited build minutes; additional push notification capacity
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for small teams. Microsoft hasn't raised prices in years, which combined with minimal updates suggests the platform may be winding down.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; typical Microsoft API rate limits apply

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API has OpenAPI spec which helps with tooling. Error messages are standard HTTP with JSON bodies. Build failure reasons are exposed via API but may require deep parsing.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Build triggers create new builds. Distribution releases are versioned and idempotent for same version. Push notifications are not idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: App Center's development is minimal — some features are deprecated or under-documented
Build time (240 min/month free) can be exhausted quickly in active development
Crash reporting API has complex query format for filtering by crash group, version, etc.
Distribution groups require manual invitation management — API can add users but they must accept
Platform-specific signing certificates (iOS provisioning profiles, Android keystore) must be pre-configured
Webhook signatures for security verification are not enforced by default
Migration to Azure Mobile Apps may be required in the future as App Center winds down
